info.parenttitle: B3.4 Electronic systems application and selection (HL only)

markdown:
<callout type="tip">Refer back to **A3.4** on circuit diagrams components & symbols.</callout>

## Circuit Arrangement


1. **Circuit arrangement** refers to how **electronic components** are **connected** to achieve a specific **function**. 
2. **Components** include **resistors, capacitors, switches, relays, diodes, transistors, operational amplifiers, integrated circuits,** and **input/output devices**. 
3. **Arrangements** can be **series, parallel,** or a **combination** of both.

### Tips for Drawing System Diagrams


1. Use **standard symbols** (as per A3.4).
2. Draw **neat, straight lines** for wires (typically horizontal/vertical).
3. Label all components clearly - include values (e.g., resistor resistance, capacitor capacitance).

![Simple circuit diagram in series.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/19adcba80e439a6f2bc81b0ddff6624d72b9275e-1566x1083.png)

<callout type="tip">* You can use **Circuit Wizard** (or similar software like Fritzing or Tinkercad Circuits) to create accurate **circuit diagrams** using standard electronic symbols. 
* It’s a helpful tool for visualizing and testing your designs before building them physically.</callout>
